channel.title: Recycling on My Personal Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Recycling on My Personal Blog

item.title: ISEQ Logo: Art from Recycled IBM Components
item.description:
Well, it’s finally been revealed during the 7th annual meeting of ISEQ Pôle d’excellence de l’Industrie des Systèmes Électroniques du Québec. This logo was ordered many months ago, following the inauguration of a site in Sherbrooke last year, where my first logos were unveiled.

The IBM Connection

Due to the proximity (and not just geographically) with IBM, Alexandra Tolza asked me to maximize IBM used parts to create that logo. This challenge pushed me to source authentic IBM components, transforming obsolete technology into a meaningful piece of art that represents the electronic systems industry in Quebec.

item.title: ATOS IT Rack Doors
item.description:
One of the last series of laser-cut metal doors that BULL/ATOS put on their Sequana racks.

As with the metal facades of the Bullion B (last post), all this has been abandoned and replaced by plastic… a pity but understandable…

These 40 doors will serve as a base for new creations, furniture, lamps and other wall decorations

item.title: ECE School Logo
item.description:
Giving visual meaning to a committed collaboration, that’s the heart of this project.

🤝 To celebrate the partnership between the High-Tech startup EXAION and the engineering school ECE, this logo was conceived as the symbol of a technical and sustainable exchange.

🔧 An original creation mixing high-tech recycling and innovative design, illustrating both the technological foundation and the educational dimension of the project.

First creation with black printed circuit boards, from mining motherboards

item.title: BULL Bench Project
item.description:
Another Era

Attention to enthusiasts with graying hair: who still has this kind of chip in their infrastructure?

What are these magical cards that were the pride of Bull?

A whole beautiful era unfortunately gone… but it can be immortalized a bit.

There must be a manager who would be happy to have this kind of memory at their Bull or Atos site or a client site.

(I can only make 2 at most, so it’s FIFO mode)

item.title: PINQ² Logo - Door Display
item.description:
Rethinking digital identity through the lens of recycling and circular design, that’s the challenge met with this project for the inauguration of this HPC and this room in winter 2024.

🔧 The PINQ logo merges modern design, high-tech upcycling and innovation to embody a strong and committed digital identity: it’s the first with 3D components (heat sinks, DIMMs) added to the cut electronic boards.

💡 This creation reaffirms that it is possible to strengthen innovation with an eco-responsible approach.

item.title: PINQ² Logo - Lenovo HPC
item.description:
Rethinking digital identity through the lens of recycling and circular design, that’s the challenge met with this project for the inauguration of this HPC and this room in winter 2024.

🔧 The PINQ logo merges modern design, high-tech upcycling and innovation to embody a strong and committed digital identity: it’s the first with 3D components (heat sinks, DIMMs) added to the cut electronic boards.

💡 This creation reaffirms that it is possible to strengthen innovation with an eco-responsible approach.

item.title: Storage Unit - ARISTA Blanking Panels
item.description:
Before ending up in a classic “metals” dumpster, these blanking panels to ensure ventilation of ARISTA chassis and these proprietary Hitachi rails (which only mount on Hitachi racks) found a second-first life in my workshop.

After transforming the panels to make them “closed” and a bit of methodology to create a series of 64 compartments… a storage unit. Warning about weight… wall mounting required.

100% metal, 100% very heavy!

item.title: Support Table for a LEGO DataCenter Model
item.description:
Certainly the largest / most beautiful of my creations. Months of gestation and sorting, disassembly, component cutting…

With the decommissioning of Cisco 6000 chassis, the reuse of old memory modules from an IBM Z, GPUs from a very old HPC and failed hard drives from another HPC. This table, given its dimensions, had to be made with a long-standing metal partner, the company Pizette.

It is in the lobby of the EDF Nanterre site, and serves as a support for a Lego model of a DC.

item.title: EXAION Logo - Rack
item.description:
The first logo on a perforated IT rack door. Validates the method of mounting spacers and the cut A0 template…

At that time, surface components were removed with a Dremel or screwdriver. So no 3D effect like in the next ones. But less risk of damaging the logo when performing rack equipment maintenance.

Still at the entrance of the server room for a guaranteed wow effect!
